<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314181508 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00217: (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 to v2 transition context

- page_id: `314181508`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/314181508/2026x+Refresh1+SysML+v1+to+v2+transition+context
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-06-25T11:41:13.977+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 to SysML v2 transition
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

##### Overview

This use case diagram provides a high-level view of the SysML v1 to SysML v2 transition process, identifying the primary stakeholders and tools involved in the migration effort.

It establishes the overall context for the transition initiative before detailing the individual migration activities described in subsequent diagrams.

##### Purpose

The purpose of the transition process is to enable organizations to systematically migrate SysML v1 projects to SysML v2 while preserving model intent, minimizing migration risks, and enabling adoption of SysML v2 modeling capabilities and practices.

##### Participants

The transition process involves multiple stakeholder groups that collaborate throughout the migration lifecycle:

- Inter-disciplinary cross-functional team. Responsible for establishing organizational transition strategies, defining migration goals, securing resources, coordinating stakeholders, and overseeing transition execution.
- Systems engineer. Responsible for evaluating migration readiness, selecting migration candidates, defining transformation configurations, performing model preparation activities, and validating transformed models.
- IT engineer. Responsible for deploying and maintaining the technical infrastructure required to support SysML v2 adoption, including modeling environments, repositories, and supporting services.
- Software developer. Responsible for adapting and maintaining custom extensions, integrations, reports, scripts, and other software components required for the SysML v2 environment .
- Partners and third parties. External organizations that may participate in migration activities, tool development, methodology development, training, or integration support.
- Tooling environment. The transition process may involve several independent tools:
- SysML v1 tool. The source modeling environment containing the existing SysML v1 projects that will be migrated.
- SysML v2 tool. The target modeling environment where transformed SysML v2 models are created, maintained, and evolved.
- SysML v1-to-v2 transformation tool. A dedicated transformation solution responsible for converting SysML v1 models into SysML v2 representations. Depending on the implementation, this capability may be integrated into modeling tools or provided as a standalone transformation service.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="SysML v1-to-v2 transition.jpg" /></ac:image></strong></p><h3>Overview</h3><p>This use case diagram provides a high-level view of the SysML v1 to SysML v2 transition process, identifying the primary stakeholders and tools involved in the migration effort.</p><p>It establishes the overall context for the transition initiative before detailing the individual migration activities described in subsequent diagrams.</p><h3>Purpose</h3><p>The purpose of the transition process is to enable organizations to systematically migrate SysML v1 projects to SysML v2 while preserving model intent, minimizing migration risks, and enabling adoption of SysML v2 modeling capabilities and practices.</p><h3>Participants</h3><p>The transition process involves multiple stakeholder groups that collaborate throughout the migration lifecycle:</p><ul><li data-uuid="bb5b7554-440d-4ae0-bf43-d2c8c8f27a72"><strong>Inter-disciplinary cross-functional team. </strong>Responsible for establishing organizational transition strategies, defining migration goals, securing resources, coordinating stakeholders, and overseeing transition execution.</li><li data-uuid="6174cc02-fa0f-48ba-8fea-56ce0c05bd45"><strong>Systems engineer. </strong>Responsible for evaluating migration readiness, selecting migration candidates, defining transformation configurations, performing model preparation activities, and validating transformed models.</li><li data-uuid="0a654d90-75cb-44a3-98ab-114c1e1a7504"><strong>IT engineer. </strong>Responsible for deploying and maintaining the technical infrastructure required to support SysML v2 adoption, including modeling environments, repositories, and supporting services.</li><li data-uuid="2ce9b679-b7cf-4491-83cb-c65296589a05"><strong>Software developer. </strong>Responsible for adapting and maintaining custom extensions, integrations, reports, scripts, and other software components required for the SysML v2 environment<strong>.</strong></li><li data-uuid="7191b27d-6f0f-4cff-851e-d8b7ef6d7a74"><strong>Partners and third parties. </strong>External organizations that may participate in migration activities, tool development, methodology development, training, or integration support.</li><li data-uuid="c8dac024-ae0a-4393-9181-e259b5aeb79e"><strong>Tooling environment. </strong>The transition process may involve several independent tools:</li><li data-uuid="7c88c00f-839e-418d-b9d1-b2439df71190"><strong>SysML v1 tool. </strong>The source modeling environment containing the existing SysML v1 projects that will be migrated.</li><li data-uuid="1a10d018-85b2-428b-bc5b-bb8dc38d43ce"><strong>SysML v2 tool. </strong>The target modeling environment where transformed SysML v2 models are created, maintained, and evolved.</li><li data-uuid="4a37fb9a-cd52-4927-b15c-bde6badaf108"><strong>SysML v1-to-v2 transformation tool. </strong>A dedicated transformation solution responsible for converting SysML v1 models into SysML v2 representations. Depending on the implementation, this capability may be integrated into modeling tools or provided as a standalone transformation service.</li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004594 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00218: (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation

- page_id: `304004594`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304004594/2026x+Refresh1+SysML+v2+Plugin+Documentation
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-06-09T07:55:00.453+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

SysML v2 is a next-generation systems modeling language. Compared to SysML v1, SysML v2 intends to provide improved precision, expressiveness, and usability of the modeling language.

To fully take advantage of SysML v2, three primary plugins are available for you:

- The SysML v2 Plugin provides core SysML v2 features and includes the SysML v2 Textual Editor, enabling you to model using both textual and graphical notations.
- The [SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2EP/?contextKey=sysml-v2-evaluation-plugin-documentation&version=2026x Refresh1) allows you to conduct static evaluations of SysML v2 models .
- The [SysML v2 Simulation Plugin](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2SP/?contextKey=sysml-v2-simulation-plugin-documentation&version=2026x Refresh1) allows you to execute SysML v2 models.

For more information, please see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Prerequisites' space=''] page.

For your convenience, you can download the**SysML v2 Quick Sheet**as a concise reference to the SysML v2 language on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Quick Sheet' space=''] page.

The following topics explain how to install and use the SysML v2 Plugin:

true

## PAGE 00220: (2026x Refresh1) SysMLConstraintsSuite

- page_id: `304006469`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006469/2026x+Refresh1+SysMLConstraintsSuite
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:30.805+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Validation > (2026x Refresh1) Predefined validation suites
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The table below lists all the validation rules in the *SysMLConstraintsSuite*, along with their messages and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Reviewing validation results' space=''].

References such as *$notVariantUsages* are used in messages to indicate elements referenced by the validation rule. In the tool, they are replaced with hyperlinks to the actual elements requiring your attention to resolve the validation failure.

| Validation rule | Validation rule message | Severity |
| --- | --- | --- |
| validateAttributeUsageFeatures | An attribute feature must be referential. Current non-referential feature(s): $featureList. | Error |
| validateAttributeDefinitionFeatures | An attribute feature must be referential. Current non-referential feature(s): $featureList. | Error |
| validateDefinitionVariationOwnedFeatureMembership | A variation must own only variants. Currently owned features that are not variants: $notVariantUsages. | Error |
| validateUsageVariationOwnedFeatureMembership | A variation must own only variants. Currently owned features that are not variants: $notVariantUsages. | Error |
| validateDefinitionVariationSpecialization | A variation must not specialize another variation. Currently specialized variation(s): $specializedVariationElements. | Error |
| validateUsageVariationSpecialization | A variation must not specialize another variation. Currently specialized variation(s): $specializedVariationElements. | Error |
| validateEventOccurrenceUsageReference | Must only reference an occurrence. Currently reference subsetted feature that is not an occurrence: $notOccurrenceReferences. | Error |
| validateExhibitStateUsageReference | Must only reference a state. Currently reference subsetted feature that is not a state: $notStateReferences. | Error |
| validatePerformActionUsageReference | Must only reference an action. Currently reference subsetted feature that is not an action: $notActionReferences. | Error |
| validateAssertConstraintUsageReference | Must only reference a constraint. Currently reference subsetted feature that is not a constraint: $notConstraintReferences. | Error |
| validateSatisfyRequirementUsageReference | Must only reference a requirement. Currently reference subsetted feature that is not a requirement: $notRequirementReferences. | Error |
| validateIncludeUseCaseUsageReference | Must only reference a use case. Currently reference subsetted feature that is not a use case: $notUseCaseReferences. | Error |
| validateRequirementDefinitionOnlyOneSubject | Only one subject is allowed. Current subjects: $subjectList. | Error |
| validateRequirementUsageOnlyOneSubject | Only one subject is allowed. Current subjects: $subjectList. | Error |
| validateCaseDefinitionOnlyOneObjective | Only one objective is allowed. Current objectives: $objectiveList. | Error |
| validateCaseUsageOnlyOneObjective | Only one objective is allowed. Current objectives: $objectiveList. | Error |
| validateCaseDefinitionOnlyOneSubject | Only one subject is allowed. Current subjects: $subjectList. | Error |
| validateCaseUsageOnlyOneSubject | Only one subject is allowed. Current subjects: $subjectList. | Error |
| validateViewDefinitionOnlyOneViewRendering | Only one renderer is allowed. Current renderers: $renderList. | Error |
| validateViewUsageOnlyOneViewRendering | Only one renderer is allowed. Current renderers: $renderList. | Error |
| validateEventOccurrenceUsageReference | Must only reference an occurrence. Currently reference subsetted feature that is not an occurrence: $notOccurrenceReference. | Error |
| validateExhibitStateUsageReference | Must only reference a state. Currently reference subsetted feature that is not a state: $notStateReference. | Error |
| validatePerformActionUsageReference | Must only reference an action. Currently reference subsetted feature that is not an action: $notActionReference. | Error |
| validateAssertConstraintUsageReference | Must only reference a constraint. Currently reference subsetted feature that is not an action: $notConstraintReference. | Error |
| validateSatisfyRequirementUsageReference | Must only reference a requirement. Currently reference subsetted feature that is not a requirement: $notRequirementReference. | Error |
| validateIncludeUseCaseUsageReference | Must only reference a use case. Currently reference subsetted feature that is not a use case: $notUseCaseReference. | Error |
| validateDefinitionVariationOwnedFeatureMembership | A variation must own only variants. Currently owned features that are not variants: $notVariantUsages. | Error |
| validateUsageVariationOwnedFeatureMembership | A variation must own only variants. Currently owned features that are not variants: $notVariantUsages. | Error |
| validateDefinitionVariationSpecialization | A variation must not specialize another variation. Currently specialized variation(s): $specializedVariationElements. | Error |
| validateUsageVariationSpecialization | A variation must not specialize another variation. Currently specialized variation(s): $specializedVariationElements. | Error |
| validateViewRenderingMembershipOwningType | Must be owned by a view. Currently owned by: $owningType. | Error |
| validateActorMembershipOwningType | Must be owned by a requirement or a case. Currently owned by: $owningType. | Error |
| validateSubjectMembershipOwningType | Must be owned by a requirement or a case. Currently owned by: $owningType. | Error |
| validateStateSubactionMembershipOwningType | Must be owned by a state. Currently owned by: $owningType. | Error |
| validateStakeholderMembershipOwningType | Must be owned by a requirement. Currently owned by: $owningType. | Error |
| validateRequirementConstraintMembershipOwningType | Must be owned by a requirement. Currently owned by: $owningType. | Error |
| validateRequirementVerificationMembershipOwningType | Must be a requirement owned by an objective. Currently owned by: $owningType. | Error |
| validateObjectiveMembershipOwningType | Must be owned by a case. Currently owned by: $owningType. | Error |
| validateWhileLoopActionUsage | Must own at least two parameters. | Error |
| validateSendActionParameters | Must own at least three parameters. | Error |
| validateAcceptActionUsageParameters | Must own at least one parameter. | Error |
| validateIfActionUsageParameters | Must own at least two parameters. | Error |
| validateForLoopActionUsageParameters | Must own exactly two parameters. Currently owned parameter(s): $parameterList.ConditionWhen there are more than 2 parameters. | Error |
| Must own exactly two parameters.ConditionWhen there are fewer than 2 parameters. |  |  |
| validateUsageVariationIsAbstract | Variation usage must be abstract. | Error |
| validateUsageIsReferential | Directed, end, or untyped usage must be referential. | Error |
| validateRequirementConstraintMembershipIsComposite | Must be composite. | Error |
| validateReferenceUsageIsReference | Must be referential. | Error |
| validatePortUsageNestedUsagesNotComposite | An owned usage that is not a port must be referential. Currently owned usages that are neither ports nor referential: $nonReferentialNotPortUsages. | Error |
| validatePortDefinitionOwnedUsagesNotComposite | An owned usage that is not a port must be referential. Currently owned usages that are neither ports nor referential: $nonReferentialNotPortUsages. | Error |
| validatePortUsageIsReference | Port not owned by another port must be referential. | Error |
| validateControlNodeIsComposite | Must be composite. | Error |
| validateAttributeUsageIsReference | Must be referential. | Error |
| validateEventOccurrenceUsageIsReference | Must be referential. | Error |
| validateTransitionUsageParameters | Must own at least one input parameter.ConditionWhen triggerAction is empty. | Error |
| Must own at least two input parameters.ConditionWhen triggerAction is not empty. |  |  |
| validateStateUsageStateSubactionKind | Only one owned 'entry' action is allowed. Currently owned 'entry' actions: $entryActions.ConditionWhen there is more than one 'entry' action. | Error |
| Only one owned 'do' action is allowed. Currently owned 'do' actions: $doActions.ConditionWhen there is more than one 'do' action. |  |  |
| Only one owned 'exit' action is allowed. Currently owned 'exit' actions: $exitActions.ConditionWhen there is more than one 'exit' action. |  |  |
| validateStateDefinitionStateSubactionKind | Only one owned 'entry' action is allowed. Currently owned 'entry' actions: $entryActions.ConditionWhen there is more than one 'entry' action. | Error |
| Only one owned 'do' action is allowed. Currently owned 'do' actions: $doActions.ConditionWhen there is more than one 'do' action. |  |  |
| Only one owned 'exit' action is allowed. Currently owned 'exit' actions: $exitActions.ConditionWhen there is more than one 'exit' action. |  |  |
| validatePartUsagePartDefinition | Must be typed by at least one part definition. | Error |
| validateConjugatedPortDefinitionConjugatedPortDefinitionIsEmpty | Must not own any conjugated port definitions. Currently conjugated port definitions: $conjugatedPortDefinitions. | Error |
| validateFlowDefinitionFlowEnds | Must not have more than two ends. Current ends: $ends. | Error |
| validateConnectionDefinitionIsSufficient | Must be sufficient. | Error |
| validateCaseDefinitionSubjectParameterPosition | The subject must be the first parameter. Current first parameter: $currentFirstParameter. | Error |
| validateCaseUsageSubjectParameterPosition | The subject must be the first parameter. Current first parameter: $currentFirstParameter. | Error |
| validateRequirementDefinitionSubjectParameterPosition | The subject must be the first parameter. Current first parameter: $currentFirstParameter. | Error |
| validateRequirementUsageSubjectParameterPosition | The subject must be the first parameter. Current first parameter: $currentFirstParameter. | Error |
| validateForLoopActionUsageLoopVariable | The first owned feature must be a reference usage. Current first owned feature: $currentFirstParameter. | Error |
| validateDefinitionVariationIsAbstract | A variation must be abstract. | Error |
| validateEnumerationDefinitionIsVariation | Must be a variation. | Error |
| validateExposeOwningNamespace | Must be owned by a view usage. | Error |
| validateExposeVisibility | Must have protected visibility. | Error |
| validateExposeIsImportAll | An expose relationship always imports all elements, regardless of visibility. | Error |
| validateFramedConcernMembershipConstraintKind | A framed concern membership must have a 'requirement' kind. | Error |
| validateRequirementVerificationMembershipKind | A requirement verification membership must have a 'requirement' kind. | Error |
| validateOccurrenceUsageIsPortion | An occurrence with a portion kind must be a portion. | Error |
| validateVariantMembershipOwningNamespace | The owning namespace of a variant membership must be a variation-point definition or usage. Current namespace: $namespace. | Error |
| validateTransitionFeatureMembershipOwningType | The owning type of a transition feature membership must be a transition usage. Current owning type: $owningType. | Error |
| validateDecisionNodeIncomingSuccessions | A decide may have at most one incoming succession. Currently has: $successions. | Error |
| validateForkNodeIncomingSuccessions | A fork may have at most one incoming succession. Currently has: $successions. | Error |
| validateJoinNodeOutgoingSuccessions | A join may have at most one outgoing succession. Currently has: $successions. | Error |
| validateMergeNodeOutgoingSuccessions | A merge may have at most one outgoing succession. Currently has: $successions. | Error |
| validateControlNodeOwningType | Must be owned by an action. | Error |
| validateAssignmentActionUsage | The target of an assignment action must be able to have time-varying values. Current target: $target. | Error |
| validateAssignmentActionUsageReferent | An assignment action must reference a feature as its target. Current target: $referent. | Error |
| validateConjugatedPortDefinitionOriginalPortDefinition | A conjugated port definition must reference the same original port definition as its port conjugator. Current conjugator: $portConjugator. | Error |
| validateStateUsageParallelSubactions | A parallel state cannot own successions or transitions. | Error |
| validateStateDefinitionParallelSubactions | A parallel state cannot own successions or transitions. | Error |
| validateTransitionFeatureMembershipEffectAction | A transition feature whose kind is 'effect' must be an action usage. | Error |
| validateTransitionFeatureMembershipGuardExpression | A transition feature whose kind is 'guard' must be an expression whose result is Boolean with multiplicity 1..1. | Error |
| validateTransitionFeatureMembershipTriggerAction | A transition feature whose kind is 'trigger' must be an accept action usage. | Error |
| validateObjectiveMembershipIsComposite | Must be composite. | Error |
| validateOccurrenceUsageIndividualDefinition | An occurrence usage must be typed by at most one individual occurrence definition. Currently typed by: $IndividualDefinitions. | Error |
| validateOccurrenceUsageIndividualUsage | An individual occurrence usage must be typed by an individual occurrence definition. Currently typed by: $definitions. | Error |
| validateOccurrenceUsagePortionKind | An occurrence usage with a portion kind must be owned by an occurrence definition or another occurrence usage. Current owning type is $owningType. | Error |
| validateCrossSubsettingCrossedFeature | The crossed feature must define exactly two chaining features, and their order must match the crossed end features. Current chaining features: $chainingFeatures. | Error |
| validateRedefinitionDirectionConformance | Direction must conform to the direction of the redefined feature. Currently redefined feature: $redefinedFeature. | Error |
| validateRedefinitionFeaturingTypes | A package-level feature cannot be redefined. Currently redefined feature: $redefinedFeature. ConditionWhen a package-level feature is redefined. | Error |
| A feature cannot redefine another feature within the same type. Currently redefined feature: $redefinedFeatures.ConditionWhen the redefining and the redefined features are owned by the same type. |  |  |
| validateSubsettingFeaturingTypes | The feature cannot access a subsetted feature. Tip: use dot notation for nesting. Non-accessible feature: $subsettedFeature. | Error |
| validateRedefinitionEndConformance | A non-end feature cannot redefine an end feature. Currently redefined end feature: $redefinedFeatures. | Error |
| validateSubsettingUniquenessConformance | A non-unique feature cannot subset a unique feature. Currently subsetted unique feature: $subsettedFeatures. | Error |
| validateSubsettingConstantConformance | A variable feature cannot subset a constant feature. Currently subsetted constant feature: $subsetedFeatures. | Error |
| validateFeatureValueOverriding | Cannot override a non-default value - the redefined feature already has a non-default value. Redefined feature(s): $redefinedFeatures. | Error |
| validateTypeOwnedMultiplicity | Only one multiplicity is allowed. Current multiplicities: $multiplicityRange. | Error |
| validateFlowPayloadFeature | Only one payload is allowed. Current payloads: $payloadFeatures. | Error |
| validateFeatureValueOverriding | Cannot override a non-default value – the redefined feature already has a non-default value. Redefined feature(s): $redefinedFeatures. | Error |
| validateReturnParameterMembershipOwningType | Must be owned by a calculation or a constraint. Currently owned by: $owningType. | Error |
| validateResultExpressionMembershipOwningType | Must be owned by a calculation or a constraint. Currently owned by: $owningType. | Error |
| validateFlowEndOwningType | Must be owned by a flow. Currently owned by: $owningType. | Error |
| validateStructureSpecialization | A structure must not specialize a behavior. Currently specialized behavior(s): $specializedBehavior. | Error |
| validateBehaviorSpecialization | A behavior must not specialize a structure. Currently specialized structure(s): $specializedStructure. | Error |
| validateSpecificationSpecificNotConjugated | A conjugated type cannot specialize another type. Currently specialized type(s): $generalType. | Error |
| validateTypeAtMostOneConjugator | Only one conjugation relationship is allowed. Current conjugation relationship(s): $ConjugationRelationships. | Error |
| validatePortDefinitionConjugatedPortDefinition | Only one owned conjugated port definition is allowed. Currently owned conjugated port definition(s): $ConjugationPortDefinitions.ConditionWhen there is more than 1 owned conjugated part definition. | Error |
| Must own a conjugated port definition.ConditionWhen there are no owned conjugated port definitions. |  |  |
| validateExpressionResultExpressionMembership | Only one expression is allowed. Current expressions: $expressionList. | Error |
| validateFunctionResultExpressionMembership | Only one expression is allowed. Current expressions: $expressionList. | Error |
| validateFeatureValueIsInitial | A non-variable feature cannot have an initial feature value. | Error |
| validateFlowEndNestedFeature | Only one owned feature is allowed. Current owned features: $ownedFeatureList.ConditionWhen there is more than one owned feature. | Error |
| Must own one feature.ConditionWhen there are no owned features. |  |  |
| validateAssociationBinarySpecialization | An association with more than two ends must not specialize $binaryLink. | Error |
| validateConnectorBinarySpecialization | A connector with more than two ends must not specialize $binaryLink. | Error |
| validateAssociationRelatedTypes | Not abstract association must have at least two related types. | Error |
| validateConnectorRelatedFeatures | Not abstract connector must have at least two related features. | Error |
| validateAssociationEndTypes | An end feature must have exactly one type. Current types: $types. | Error |
| validateBindingConnectorIsBinary | Must have exactly two related features. Current related features: $ends. | Error |
| validateEndFeatureMembershipIsEnd | Must be an end feature. | Error |
| validateFlowEndIsEnd | Must be an end feature. | Error |
| validateFeatureEndNoDirection | An end feature must not have a direction. | Error |
| validateFeatureEndIsConstant | An end feature that is variable must also be constant. | Error |
| validateFeatureEndNotDerivedAbstractCompositeOrPortion | An end feature must not be derived, abstract, composite, or have a portion kind value. | Error |
| validateImportTopLevelVisibility | A top-level import must have private visibility. | Error |
| validateExpressionResultParameterMembership | Must own exactly one result. Currently owned results: $results. | Error |
| ValidateFunctionResultParameterMembership | Must own exactly one result. Currently owned results: $results. | Error |
| validateFeaturePortionNotVariable | A feature with portion kind must not be variable. | Error |
| validateClassifierMultiplicityDomain | A multiplicity owned by a classifier must not have any featuring types. | Error |
| validateFeatureMultiplicityDomain | A multiplicity owned by a feature must have a featuring type the same as the feature itself. | Error |
| validateMultiplicityRangeBounds | The lower bound (if any) and upper bound expressions must be the first owned members. Currently owned members before bounds: $ownedMembers. | Error |
| validateMultiplicityRangeBoundResultTypes | Multiplicity range bounds must be integer-valued and non-negative when evaluable. | Error |
| validateDataTypeSpecialization | A data type must not specialize a class or an association. Currently specialized class(es) and/or association(s): $specializedClassAssociation. | Error |
| validateClassSpecialization | A classifier must not specialize a data type or an association. Currently specialized data type(s) and/or association(s): $specializedDataTypeAssociation. | Error |
| validateFeatureChainingFeatureConformance | $chainingFeature must be featured within $previousChainingFeature. | Error |
| validateFeatureChainingFeatureNotOne | A feature must have either no chaining features or more than one. | Error |
| validateFeatureChainingFeaturesNotSelf | A feature cannot be one of its own chaining features. | Error |
| validateFeatureConstantIsVariable | A constant feature must be variable. | Error |
| validateFeatureIsVariable | A variable feature must directly or indirectly specialize Occurrences::Occurrence. | Error |
| validateFeatureValueIsInitial | A non-variable feature cannot have an initial feature value. | Error |
| validateCollectExpressionOperator | The operator must be 'collect'. Current operator: '$operator'. | Error |
| validateFeatureChainExpressionOperator | The operator must be '.'. Current operator: '$operator'. | Error |
| validateIndexExpressionOperator | The operator must be '#'. Current operator: '$operator'. | Error |
| validateSelectExpressionOperator | The operator must be 'select'. Current operator: '$operator'. | Error |
| validateInstantiationExpressionInstantiatedType | An instantiation expression must have an instantiated type. | Error |
| validateParameterMembershipParameterDirection | The member of a parameter membership must have the '$expectedDirection' direction. Current direction: '$memberDirection'. | Error |
| validateParameterMembershipOwningType | The owning type of a parameter membership must be a behavior, step, or result parameter of a constructor expression. Current owning type: $owningType. | Error |
| validateInstantiationExpressionResult | An expression must own its result parameter. | Error |
| validateFeatureReferenceExpressionResult | An expression must own its result parameter. | Error |
| validateAnnotationAnnotatedElementOwnership | An annotation must own its annotating element if and only if it is owned by the annotated element. | Error |
| validateAnnotationAnnotatingElement | An annotation must own its annotating element or be owned by one, but not both. | Error |
| validateConstructorExpressionNoDuplicateFeatureRedefinition | A constructor expression must not redefine the same feature more than once. Current features redefined more than once: $redefinedFeatures. | Error |
| validateConstructorExpressionOwnedFeatures | A constructor expression must not own any features other than its result. Currently owned features other than the result: $ownedFeatures. | Error |
| validateElementFilterMembershipConditionIsBoolean | A condition result must directly or indirectly specialize ScalarValues::Boolean. | Error |
| validateFeatureReferenceExpressionReferentIsFeature | The first non-parameter owned member must be a feature.Current first owned member: $firstMember. | Error |
| validateInvocationExpressionInstantiatedType | The instantiated type of an invocation expression must be a behavior or a feature typed by exactly one behavior. Current instantiated type(s): $instantiatedTypes. | Error |
| validateInvocationExpressionOwnedFeatures | All owned features of an invocation expression, other than its result, must have the 'in' direction. Currently owned features with other directions: $ownedFeaturesWithoutInDirection. | Error |
| validateInvocationExpressionParameterRedefinition | Each input parameter of an invocation expression must redefine exactly one input parameter of its instantiated type. Input parameters with zero or multiple redefinitions: $inputParameters. | Error |
| validateFeatureEndMultiplicity | End feature must have a multiplicity of [1]. | Error |
| validateFeatureOwnedReferenceSubsetting | Only one reference subsetting (::>) is allowed. Current reference subsetting: $referenceSubsettedList. | Error |
| validateFeatureOwnedCrossSubsetting | Only one cross subsetting (=>) is allowed. Current cross subsettings: $crossSubsettedList. | Error |
| validateRedefinitionEndConformance | A non-end feature cannot redefine an end feature. Currently redefined end feature: $redefinedFeatures. | Error |
| validateSubsettingUniquenessConformance | A non-unique feature cannot subset a unique feature. Currently subsetted unique feature: $subsettedFeatures. | Error |
| validateSubsettingConstantConformance | A variable feature cannot subset a constant feature. Currently subsetted constant feature: $subsetedFeatures. | Error |
| validateInvocationExpressionInstantiatedType | The instantiated type of an invocation expression must be a behavior or a feature typed by exactly one behavior. Current instantiated type(s): $instantiatedTypes. | Error |
| validateInvocationExpressionOwnedFeatures | All owned features of an invocation expression, other than its result, must have the 'in' direction. Currently owned features with other directions: $ownedFeaturesWithoutInDirection. | Error |
| validateInvocationExpressionParameterRedefinition | Each input parameter of an invocation expression must redefine exactly one input parameter of its instantiated type. Input parameters with zero or multiple redefinitions: $inputParameters. | Error |
| validateInvocationExpressionNoDuplicateParameterRedefinition | An invocation expression must not have multiple owned features redefining the same feature of its instantiated type. Current duplicate redefinitions: $duplicateRedefinedFeatures redefines $redefinedFeature. | Error |
| validateMetadataFeatureBody | A metadata feature must be unnamed, redefine exactly one feature, have only model-level evaluable values, and only own features with the same restrictions. Invalid feature(s) of metadata: $InvalidFeaturesOfMetadata. | Error |
| validateMetadataFeatureAnnotatedElement | Must annotate an element whose metaclass is $AllowedMetaclasses.ConditionThere is only one ref element that subsets/redefines annotatedElement in the metadata definition. | Error |
| Must annotate an element whose metaclass is one of: $AllowedMetaclasses.ConditionThere are multiple ref elements that subset annotatedElement in the metadata definition. |  |  |
| Non-matching annotated element(s): $NonMatchingElement.ConditionThe same metadata usage annotates multiple elements. |  |  |
| validateMetadataAccessExpressionReferencedElement | A metadata access expression must have at least one member that is not owned by a feature membership. | Error |
| validateMetadataFeatureMetaclass | A metadata feature must be typed by exactly one metaclass. Currently typed by: $types. | Error |
| validateMetadataFeatureMetaclassNotAbstract | A metadata feature must be typed by a non-abstract metaclass. Currently typed by: $type. | Error |
| validateCrossSubsettingCrossingFeature | The crossing feature must be an end feature owned by a type with at least two end features. | Error |
| validateFeatureCrossFeatureSpecialization | The owned cross feature must specialize the cross features of all redefined features. | Error |
| validateFeatureCrossFeatureType | The owned cross feature must have the same types as this feature.Feature type(s): $featureType. Cross feature type(s): $crossFeatureTypes. | Error |
| validateFeatureChainExpressionConformance | The target feature $targetFeature must be featured within $argumentResult. | Error |

Currently owned usages that are neither ports nor referential: $nonReferentialNotPortUsages.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validatePortDefinitionOwnedUsagesNotComposite</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An owned usage that is not a port must be referential. Currently owned usages that are neither ports nor referential: $nonReferentialNotPortUsages.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td>validatePortUsageIsReference</td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Port not owned by another port must be referential.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td>validateControlNodeIsComposite</td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be composite.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td>validateAttributeUsageIsReference</td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be referential. </span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td>validateEventOccurrenceUsageIsReference</td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be referential. </span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">validateTransitionUsageParameters</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must own at least one input parameter.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b913dea1-791d-45de-9350-d891391fe983"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">When triggerAction is empty.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td rowspan="2">Error</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must own at least two input parameters.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="216a2b23-aa13-408b-9804-8dd98fb3e9b4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">When triggerAction is not empty.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="3">validateStateUsageStateSubactionKind</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one owned 'entry' action is allowed. Currently owned 'entry' actions: $entryActions.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="147e17d1-5013-46aa-ba7d-2ac60d8faea1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">When there is more than one 'entry' action.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td rowspan="3">Error</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one owned 'do' action is allowed. Currently owned 'do' actions: $doActions.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4d03e99-2433-42e7-a814-e84a3681f506"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">When there is more than one 'do' action.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one owned 'exit' action is allowed. Currently owned 'exit' actions: $exitActions.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="401d62c0-c69d-4521-b5c4-b64f02b30745"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">When there is more than one 'exit' action.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="3">validateStateDefinitionStateSubactionKind</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one owned 'entry' action is allowed. Currently owned 'entry' actions: $entryActions.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="643f787e-7038-4a80-9fc2-6cefcf9f79d8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">When there is more than one 'entry' action.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td rowspan="3">Error</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one owned 'do' action is allowed. Currently owned 'do' actions: $doActions.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9e010112-29cb-4c40-bf16-22093aa6f5b4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">When there is more than one 'do' action.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one owned 'exit' action is allowed. Currently owned 'exit' actions: $exitActions.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76b6d8bf-c42a-4da0-8d37-441d3bee5490"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">When there is more than one 'exit' action.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td>validatePartUsagePartDefinition</td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be typed by at least one part definition.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td>validateConjugatedPortDefinitionConjugatedPortDefinitionIsEmpty</td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must not own any conjugated port definitions. Currently conjugated port definitions: $conjugatedPortDefinitions.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td>validateFlowDefinitionFlowEnds</td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must not have more than two ends. Current ends: $ends.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td>validateConnectionDefinitionIsSufficient</td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be sufficient.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td>validateCaseDefinitionSubjectParameterPosition</td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The subject must be the first parameter. Current first parameter: $currentFirstParameter.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateCaseUsageSubjectParameterPosition</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The subject must be the first parameter. Current first parameter: $currentFirstParameter.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateRequirementDefinitionSubjectParameterPosition</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The subject must be the first parameter. Current first parameter: $currentFirstParameter.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateRequirementUsageSubjectParameterPosition</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The subject must be the first parameter. Current first parameter: $currentFirstParameter.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateForLoopActionUsageLoopVariable</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The first owned feature must be a reference usage. Current first owned feature: $currentFirstParameter.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateDefinitionVariationIsAbstract</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A variation must be abstract.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateEnumerationDefinitionIsVariation</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be a variation.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateExposeOwningNamespace</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be owned by a view usage.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateExposeVisibility</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must have protected visibility.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateExposeIsImportAll</p></td><td>An expose relationship always imports all elements, regardless of visibility.</td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateFramedConcernMembershipConstraintKind</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A framed concern membership must have a 'requirement' kind. </span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateRequirementVerificationMembershipKind</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A requirement verification membership must have a 'requirement' kind.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateOccurrenceUsageIsPortion</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An occurrence with a portion kind must be a portion.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateVariantMembershipOwningNamespace</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The owning namespace of a variant membership must be a variation-point definition or usage. Current namespace: $namespace.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateTransitionFeatureMembershipOwningType</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The owning type of a transition feature membership must be a transition usage. Current owning type: $owningType.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateDecisionNodeIncomingSuccessions</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A decide may have at most one incoming succession. Currently has: $successions.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateForkNodeIncomingSuccessions</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A fork may have at most one incoming succession. Currently has: $successions.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateJoinNodeOutgoingSuccessions</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A join may have at most one outgoing succession. Currently has: $successions.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateMergeNodeOutgoingSuccessions</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A merge may have at most one outgoing succession. Currently has: $successions. </span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateControlNodeOwningType</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be owned by an action.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateAssignmentActionUsage</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The target of an assignment action must be able to have time-varying values. Current target: $target.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateAssignmentActionUsageReferent</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An assignment action must reference a feature as its target. Current target: $referent.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateConjugatedPortDefinitionOriginalPortDefinition</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A conjugated port definition must reference the same original port definition as its port conjugator. Current conjugator: $portConjugator.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateStateUsageParallelSubactions</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A parallel state cannot own successions or transitions.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateStateDefinitionParallelSubactions</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A parallel state cannot own successions or transitions.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateTransitionFeatureMembershipEffectAction</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A transition feature whose kind is 'effect' must be an action usage.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateTransitionFeatureMembershipGuardExpression</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A transition feature whose kind is 'guard' must be an expression whose result is Boolean with multiplicity 1..1. </span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateTransitionFeatureMembershipTriggerAction</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A transition feature whose kind is 'trigger' must be an accept action usage.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateObjectiveMembershipIsComposite</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be composite.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateOccurrenceUsageIndividualDefinition</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An occurrence usage must be typed by at most one individual occurrence definition. Currently typed by: $IndividualDefinitions.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateOccurrenceUsageIndividualUsage</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An individual occurrence usage must be typed by an individual occurrence definition. Currently typed by: $definitions.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateOccurrenceUsagePortionKind</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An occurrence usage with a portion kind must be owned by an occurrence definition or another occurrence usage. Current owning type is $owningType.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateCrossSubsettingCrossedFeature</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The crossed feature must define exactly two chaining features, and their order must match the crossed end features. Current chaining features: $chainingFeatures.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateRedefinitionDirectionConformance</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Direction must conform to the direction of the redefined feature. Currently redefined feature: $redefinedFeature.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><p>validateRedefinitionFeaturingTypes</p><p><br /></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A package-level feature cannot be redefined. Currently redefined feature: $redefinedFeature. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d10dfc27-b3de-40e8-bed7-a60eb27906ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When a package-level feature is redefined.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td rowspan="2">Error</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A feature cannot redefine another feature within the same type. Currently redefined feature: $redefinedFeatures.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9fb6a449-7548-4088-b232-4eb0a094f422"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">When the redefining and the redefined features are owned by the same type.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateSubsettingFeaturingTypes</span></p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The feature cannot access a subsetted feature. Tip: use dot notation for nesting. Non-accessible feature: $subsettedFeature.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateRedefinitionEndConformance</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A non-end feature cannot redefine an end feature. Currently redefined end feature: $redefinedFeatures.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateSubsettingUniquenessConformance</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A non-unique feature cannot subset a unique feature. Currently subsetted unique feature: $subsettedFeatures.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateSubsettingConstantConformance</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A variable feature cannot subset a constant feature. Currently subsetted constant feature: $subsetedFeatures.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateFeatureValueOverriding</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Cannot override a non-default value - the redefined feature already has a non-default value. Redefined feature(s): $redefinedFeatures.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateTypeOwnedMultiplicity</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one multiplicity is allowed. Current multiplicities: $multiplicityRange.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateFlowPayloadFeature</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one payload is allowed. Current payloads: $payloadFeatures.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateFeatureValueOverriding</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Cannot override a non-default value – the redefined feature already has a non-default value. Redefined feature(s): $redefinedFeatures.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateReturnParameterMembershipOwningType</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be owned by a calculation or a constraint. Currently owned by: $owningType.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateResultExpressionMembershipOwningType</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be owned by a calculation or a constraint. Currently owned by: $owningType.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateFlowEndOwningType</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be owned by a flow. Currently owned by: $owningType.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateStructureSpecialization</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A structure must not specialize a behavior. Currently specialized behavior(s): $specializedBehavior.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateBehaviorSpecialization</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A behavior must not specialize a structure. Currently specialized structure(s): $specializedStructure.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateSpecificationSpecificNotConjugated</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A conjugated type cannot specialize another type. Currently specialized type(s): $generalType.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateTypeAtMostOneConjugator</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one conjugation relationship is allowed. Current conjugation relationship(s): $ConjugationRelationships.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><p>validatePortDefinitionConjugatedPortDefinition</p><p><br /></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one owned conjugated port definition is allowed. Currently owned conjugated port definition(s): $ConjugationPortDefinitions.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c9032f3-b794-42f6-9492-4a05a1609f82"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When there is more than 1 owned conjugated part definition.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td rowspan="2">Error</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must own a conjugated port definition.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6480dffd-1f27-46ee-aeab-93d53287bc9d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When there are no owned conjugated port definitions.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>validateExpressionResultExpressionMembership</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one expression is allowed. Current expressions: $expressionList.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateFunctionResultExpressionMembership</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one expression is allowed. Current expressions: $expressionList.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateFeatureValueIsInitial</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A non-variable feature cannot have an initial feature value.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><p>validateFlowEndNestedFeature</p><p><br /></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one owned feature is allowed. Current owned features: $ownedFeatureList.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b157907-d767-482c-be88-a8d6f117762c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When there is more than one owned feature.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td rowspan="2">Error</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must own one feature.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab2ba156-846b-416a-ae42-3cb5b3d7b1b9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When there are no owned features.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>validateAssociationBinarySpecialization</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An association with more than two ends must not specialize $binaryLink.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateConnectorBinarySpecialization</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A connector with more than two ends must not specialize $binaryLink.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateAssociationRelatedTypes</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Not abstract association must have at least two related types.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateConnectorRelatedFeatures</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Not abstract connector must have at least two related features.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateAssociationEndTypes</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An end feature must have exactly one type. Current types: $types.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateBindingConnectorIsBinary</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must have exactly two related features. Current related features: $ends.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateEndFeatureMembershipIsEnd</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be an end feature.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFlowEndIsEnd</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be an end feature.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureEndNoDirection</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An end feature must not have a direction.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureEndIsConstant</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An end feature that is variable must also be constant.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureEndNotDerivedAbstractCompositeOrPortion</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An end feature must not be derived, abstract, composite, or have a portion kind value.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateImportTopLevelVisibility</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A top-level import must have private visibility.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateExpressionResultParameterMembership</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must own exactly one result. Currently owned results: $results.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">ValidateFunctionResultParameterMembership</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must own exactly one result. Currently owned results: $results.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeaturePortionNotVariable</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A feature with portion kind must not be variable.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateClassifierMultiplicityDomain</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A multiplicity owned by a classifier must not have any featuring types.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureMultiplicityDomain</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A multiplicity owned by a feature must have a featuring type the same as the feature itself.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateMultiplicityRangeBounds</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The lower bound (if any) and upper bound expressions must be the first owned members. Currently owned members before bounds: $ownedMembers.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateMultiplicityRangeBoundResultTypes</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Multiplicity range bounds must be integer-valued and non-negative when evaluable.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateDataTypeSpecialization</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A data type must not specialize a class or an association. Currently specialized class(es) and/or association(s): $specializedClassAssociation.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateClassSpecialization</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A classifier must not specialize a data type or an association. Currently specialized data type(s) and/or association(s): $specializedDataTypeAssociation.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureChainingFeatureConformance</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">$chainingFeature must be featured within $previousChainingFeature.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureChainingFeatureNotOne</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A feature must have either no chaining features or more than one.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureChainingFeaturesNotSelf</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A feature cannot be one of its own chaining features.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureConstantIsVariable</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A constant feature must be variable.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureIsVariable</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A variable feature must directly or indirectly specialize Occurrences::Occurrence.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureValueIsInitial</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A non-variable feature cannot have an initial feature value.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateCollectExpressionOperator</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The operator must be 'collect'. Current operator: '$operator'.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureChainExpressionOperator</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The operator must be '.'. element whose metaclass is one of: $AllowedMetaclasses.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a036f41-69db-46be-9e59-3a0e855d4078"><ac:parameter
