<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004974 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00221: (2026x Refresh1) Tabular view columns

- page_id: `304004974`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304004974/2026x+Refresh1+Tabular+view+columns
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-05-06T22:29:29.627+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Creating views > (2026x Refresh1) Tabular views > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Tabular views contain columns used to represent specific information about the exposed elements. See the information on this page to learn about table columns before you start [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns' space=''].

##### Columns in tabular view definitions and usages

Tabular views and their definitions can differ in the table columns they contain:

- Tabular view definitions:
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Tabular views' space=''] come with predefined columns.
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom tabular view definitions' space=''] contain only the columns you specify.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating tabular views' space=''] inherit columns from the view definitions they are defined by. You can also specify custom columns for them.

##### Column kinds

Currently, there are 2 model-based table column kinds available for modeling. The columns you create for a tabular view usage or definition must be defined by one of these column definitions, depending on the column's purpose. The table below lists:

- Column kinds and their descriptions.
- Relevant information regarding cell type according to column kind.
  - The cell type enables correct data representation and correct cell value type (e.g., String, Boolean, etc.) suggestions upon cell editing.
- Predefined attributes applicable to each column kind.

| Column | Description | Cells | Cell attributes | Column attributes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ColumnByFeatureView | Dedicated to meta-feature, or user-defined feature, value retrieval. It requires the expression to return only one meta-feature/user-defined feature, e.g., Element::declaredName or FeatureValue::value. The expression is specified for columnFeature.See procedure: on the page. on the page. | For ColumnByFeatureView, the cell type is calculated out of the meta-feature or user-defined feature and does not need to be specified explicitly. | maxDisplayedValues. It allows you to modify the default limit for displayed values in multi-valued table cells.Available values: Integer. If undefined or <=0, the attribute reverts to the default value.The default value is 10. >]]> | isMultiline. It allows you to specify whether single-valued string cells should be displayed in a multiline format.Available values: Boolean.The default value is false.pinning. It allows you to pin the column to the left or the right side of the table.Available values: left/right.By default, no column is pinned.sorting. It allows you to sort table rows in ascending or descending order according to the selected column. If the sorting is specified for multiple columns, use the sortingOrder attribute to specify the sorting order priority for the columns.Available values: asc/desc. If undefined or null, the column is not sorted.By default, the attribute value is undefined.sortingOrder. It allows you to specify the sorting order priority for the columns with the sorting attribute. The table rows are ordered for all columns based on the order specified using this attribute, e.g., a column with sortingOrder set to 0 will take the highest priority.If the sorting attribute is specified but the sortingOrder is not, sorting is applied without any order. Available values: Integer.By default, the attribute value is undefined.width. It allows you to specify the column width, where the integer value is interpreted in pixels. Available values: Integer. If undefined or null, the column width is responsive.By default, the attribute value is undefined.>]]> |
| ColumnByExpressionView | Dedicated to complex model queries. For example, when the value of the owned attribute needs to be retrieved, the feature chain expression is needed to access this information.See procedures: on the page. on the page. | For ColumnByExpressionView, the cell type is specified by defining the cell rendering with the appropriate rendering definition. It works as a kind of filter for the type of wanted expression results.The default cell rendering definition is StringCellRendering.Rendering definitionsrendering def BooleanCellRendering :> TableCellRendering;rendering def ElementCellRendering :> TableCellRendering;rendering def EnumCellRendering :> TableCellRendering;rendering def IntegerCellRendering :> TableCellRendering; rendering def MetaobjectCellRendering :> TableCellRendering;rendering def RealCellRendering :> TableCellRendering;rendering def StringCellRendering :> TableCellRendering;rendering def TableCellRendering { calc getValue { in rowElement [0..1] : Metaobjects::Metaobject; } attribute isMultiValued [0..1] : ScalarValues::Boolean; } |  |  |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;">Tabular views contain columns <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a43b3e01-79af-434e-9dda-e1791f97df98">used to represent specific information about the exposed elements</ac:inline-comment-marker>. See the information on this page to learn about table columns before you start <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[modeling them]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><h3 style="text-align: left;">Columns in tabular view definitions and usages</h3><p style="text-align: left;">Tabular views and their definitions can differ in the table columns they contain:</p><ul><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="3ec94e1c-e1e5-45f5-93a9-452bcb101955">Tabular view definitions:<ul><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="cf4fd0b4-cf7c-4e77-a82b-9ae39821bac9"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Predefined tabular view definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> come with predefined columns.</li><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="a1e61253-c114-4172-9b26-27e4819b9ceb"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Custom tabular view definitions" /></ac:link> contain only the columns you specify. </li></ul></li><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="e485e8a4-807d-4d11-9586-878b40e68064"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Tabular view usages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> inherit columns from the view definitions they are defined by. You can also specify custom columns for them.</li></ul><h3 style="text-align: left;">Column kinds</h3><p style="text-align: left;">Currently, there are 2 model-based table column kinds available for modeling. The columns you create for a tabular view usage or definition must be defined by one of these column definitions, depending on the column's purpose. The table below lists:</p><ul><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="5df54d9b-131e-4645-abd4-b5b1dc18380e">Column kinds and their descriptions.</li><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="ba5b3521-0c18-45e8-b7e4-fb4b5ca0a431">Relevant information regarding cell type according to column kind.<br /><ul><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="5d2bc526-c693-4cd4-b59e-066d811217b8"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The cell type enables correct data representation and <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="00a78496-3366-4701-b4cf-6b2f70e0c1bb">correct cell value type (e.g., String, Boolean, etc.) suggestions upon cell editing.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></li></ul></li><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="1defea6a-2f57-4371-9820-62e256f8c265">Predefined attributes applicable to each column kind.</li></ul><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Column</th><th scope="col">Description</th><th scope="col">Cells</th><th scope="col">Cell attributes</th><th scope="col">Column attributes</th></tr><tr><td><em>ColumnByFeatureView</em></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">Dedicated to meta-feature, or user-defined feature, value retrieval. It requires the expression to return only one meta-feature/user-defined feature, e.g., </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">Element::declaredName </ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">or </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">FeatureValue::value</ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">. The expression is specified for </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">columnFeature</ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See procedure:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="c3a0debc-b22a-4053-9210-9cd6bd34da4d"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Adding table columns by feature"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Adding table columns by feature]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Modeling tabular view columns]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></li><li data-uuid="d905d74f-f2b5-4b39-a3f7-e97267d1a58a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Adding table columns by feature"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating custom tabular view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Adding table columns by feature]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating custom tabular view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating custom tabular view definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></li></ul></td><td><ul><li data-uuid="250b3a27-0a06-4f19-84f9-e753e9ee9773"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>For <em>ColumnByFeatureView</em>, the cell type is calculated out of the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="250e72b1-5f1b-468a-8555-495793ba82f6"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a0670dd4-96e3-4cbe-8b1b-30ab4b63c96b">meta-feature or user-defined feature</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker> </span>and does not need to be specified explicitly.</span></span></li></ul></td><td rowspan="2"><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span><em>maxDisplayedValues</em>. It allows you to modify the default limit for displayed values in multi-valued table cells.</span></span><ul><li data-uuid="b8f1362d-9a81-4f7c-a13e-2639e770d047"><span>Available values</span>: Integer. If undefined or &lt;=0, the attribute reverts to the default value.</li><li data-uuid="1838f934-d3fa-4c24-901b-dd0a4a2fe6ee"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The default value is 10. <br /><ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the value count in multi-valued cells"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing tabular view cells" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[See procedure >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></li></ul></li></ul><div class="content-wrapper"><span> </span></div></td><td rowspan="2"><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li><span><em>isMultiline</em>. It allows you to specify whether single-valued string cells should be displayed in a multiline format.</span><ul><li data-uuid="bc29d39f-3592-4062-911f-fccc9f984e88"><span>Available values: Boolean.<br /></span></li><li data-uuid="e5a1a894-050a-4aea-9e39-030b46fca172"><span>The default value is <em>false</em>.<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="050b80f8-0957-4889-a70c-e218a53a2d56"><span><em>pinning. </em>It allows you to pin the column to the left or the right side of the table.</span><ul><li data-uuid="464e3604-b298-42ce-bae4-9a7de24e0fea"><span>Available values: <em>left/right</em>.</span></li><li data-uuid="2ec08fba-92ec-40b7-913d-ea770e13a5a3"><span>By default, no column is pinned.<br /><br /></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="8044834c-b16f-4910-aed0-925d09c8e144"><span><em>sorting. </em>It allows you to sort table rows in ascending or descending order according to the selected column. </span><ul><li data-uuid="47ee0e81-841b-4d04-998a-92fc618f606b"><span>If the sorting is specified for multiple columns, use the <em>sortingOrder </em>attribute to specify the sorting order priority for the columns.<br /></span></li><li data-uuid="4d754b88-2e74-4f38-80ff-27472041ea45"><span>Available values: <em>asc/desc</em>. </span>If undefined or <em>null</em>, the column is not sorted.</li><li data-uuid="8044834c-b16f-4910-aed0-925d09c8e144"><span>By default, the attribute value is undefined.<br /><br /></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="b142151b-568b-4e12-840a-f6cfee9f6126"><span><em>sortingOrder. </em>It allows you to specify the sorting order priority for the columns with the <em>sorting </em>attribute. The table rows are ordered for all columns based on the order specified using this attribute, e.g., a column with <em>sortingOrder </em>set to <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7ba171af-9fb8-46af-850c-44ef9e0f8a2e">0</ac:inline-comment-marker> will take the highest priority.</span><ul><li data-uuid="0b30f7da-bc77-4c06-9594-3ed99b9d2457"><span>If the <em>sorting </em>attribute is specified but the <em>sortingOrder </em>is not, <em>sorting </em>is applied without any order. </span></li><li data-uuid="fd67a89a-2785-4618-84f2-788ab3b58952"><span>Available values</span>: Integer.</li><li data-uuid="ac1efaa8-c5f9-4a64-b78c-1fca1beb6ed3"><span>By default, the attribute value is undefined.</span></li></ul></li></ul><ul><li data-uuid="b705a1a7-8a59-479e-8d58-a4b2bcd2450a"><span><em>width.</em> It allows you to specify the column width, where the integer value is interpreted in pixels. </span><ul><li data-uuid="dd35de50-396d-48f9-b703-4277309c7854"><span>Available values</span>: Integer. If undefined or <em>null</em>, the column width is responsive.</li><li data-uuid="6fc21655-e1f7-43b7-a323-d261ad48aa34"><span>By default, the attribute value is undefined.</span></li></ul></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying table column attributes"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[See procedure >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><em>ColumnByExpressionView</em></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Dedicated to complex model queries. For example, when the value of the owned attribute needs to be retrieved, the feature chain expression is needed to access this information.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See procedures:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="efbc10c9-cb1b-4c4a-af70-22d85bddb31e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Adding table columns by expression"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Adding table columns by expression]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Modeling tabular view columns]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></li><li data-uuid="61a64c47-96db-4a3d-a711-d1ded97f9f28"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Adding table columns by expression"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating custom tabular view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Adding table columns by expression]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating custom tabular view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating custom tabular view definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></li></ul></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li data-uuid="3dec134c-fe7c-4ffb-8ebd-e3facc5f3402"><span>For <em>ColumnByExpressionView</em>, the cell type is specified by defining the cell rendering with the appropriate rendering definition. It works as a kind of filter for the type of <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2e4eec76-dcf8-425b-bad2-7d6ef344d3c5">wanted expression results</ac:inline-comment-marker>.<br /></span><ul><li data-uuid="1a979d84-132b-43f5-b781-82c63a6c75ae"><span>The default cell rendering definition is <em>StringCellRendering</em>.</span></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a4f5ac18-8795-4230-a5e5-cab99bbec49b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Rendering definitions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d29fa686-5aed-4181-8547-e15c52fcd897"><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">BooleanCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ElementCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">EnumCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">IntegerCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MetaobjectCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">RealCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">StringCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;<br /></span></p><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TableCellRendering</span><span> </span>{<br />   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">calc</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">getValue</span><span> </span>{<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rowElement</span><span> </span>[<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span>] : Metaobjects::Metaobject; }<br />   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isMultiValued</span><span> </span>[<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span>] : ScalarValues::Boolean; <br />}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table><p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005123 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00222: (2026x Refresh1) Tabular views

- page_id: `304005123`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005123/2026x+Refresh1+Tabular+views
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-05-06T21:57:23.057+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Creating views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Tabular views (i.e., tables) allow you to represent and modify information about the model elements and/or relationships between them.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Tabular views

Specific tabular views you create in the modeling tool are tabular view usages defined by appropriate standard predefined tabular view definitions.

- For tabular view creation procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating tabular views' space=''] page.
  - Additionally, you can create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom tabular view definitions' space=''] .
- You can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Open in Table' space=''] functionality to view element content in a table without creating a view element in the model.

There are multiple predefined tabular view definitions available in the modeling tool, each designed for specific purposes:

| View definitions | Description | Columns | Availability |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Standard predefined tabular view definitions |  |  |  |
| Generic Table | Standard predefined multi-purpose generic tabular view for element representation and modeling. | Declared NameDeclared Short NameDocumentationQualified NameOwnerOwned Element | Via Textual EditorVia the Create View command |
| Requirements Table | Standard predefined tabular view for requirements representation and modeling.It subclassifies the NonStandardLibraryElementFilter view definition, which includes multiple filters that exclude various standard library elements that are unlikely to be relevant for display in views. | Declared NameReq IDDocumentation |  |

##### Tabular view areas

Views are displayed in the View Editor tab.

- Table toolbar allows you to quickly access the options for managing data in the table.
  - See the page.
- Grid displays data according to the exposed elements and their filters.
  - See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in tabular views' space=''] page. The grid consists of:
  - Rows that appear along the side of the table, and are labeled using numbers starting with 1. Each table row represents an element .
    - See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing tabular view rows' space=''] page.
  - Columns that appear along the top of the table, and are labeled according to the specified metadata feature or named by hand. Each table column represents specific element data.
    - You can also modify tabular view columns.
      - See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns' space=''] page.
    - Column cells can be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns' space=''] .
  - Cells are the intersections of every row and column, which represent element data values.
    - See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing tabular view cells' space=''] page.
- Table info area displays table information.

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### Tabular view areas presented in a standard predefined tabular 'Generic Table' view.

###### Tabular view toolbar buttons

The table below shows the buttons available in the tabular view toolbar.

| Button | Name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Refresh | Click the button to update the displayed table content. |
|  | Open in Textual Editor | Click the button to open and highlight the table's textual notation in the Textual Editor. In a , the Textual Editor highlights all the content displayed in the table. |
|  | Add New | Clicking the button displays a dropdown menu for selecting an element to be created in the model and displayed in the table. >]]> |
|  | Delete | Clicking the button deletes the selected row elements from the table and the model. >]]> |
|  | Remove From Table | Clicking the button removes the selected row elements from the table, but not from the model. This is achieved by filtering out the selected element from the table. >]]>The button is unavailable in . |
|  | Columns | Clicking the button displays a dropdown menu for hiding/displaying table columns. It also contains a command for opening the Configure Table Columns dialog. >]]>The button is unavailable in . |
|  | Options | Clicking the button displays a dropdown menu for managing the table's display mode (e.g., Complete Tree, Compact Tree, List). >]]> |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Tabular views (i.e., tables) allow you to <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">represent and modify information about the model elements and/or relationships between them. </span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="tabular_view_example.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Tabular views</h3><p>Specific tabular views you create in the modeling tool are tabular view usages defined by appropriate standard predefined tabular view definitions.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0cf37da3-98b1-40e6-8eed-65defb6e9ba0"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>For tabular view creation procedures, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating tabular views" /></ac:link> page.<ul><li data-uuid="56878028-d135-43f1-849c-a386d8e38c84"><span>Additionally, you can create </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Custom tabular view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[custom tabular view definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="49064e7b-224f-483d-97a7-686df766fd83">You can use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Open in Table" /></ac:link> functionality to view element content in a table without creating a view element in the model.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">There are multiple predefined tabular view definitions available in the modeling tool, each designed for specific purposes:</span></p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 87.4378%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 25.5927%;" /><col style="width: 45.2137%;" /><col style="width: 16.3509%;" /><col style="width: 12.8674%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" style="text-align: center;width: 8.99482%;" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"><strong title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">View definitions</span></strong></td><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" style="text-align: center;width: 38.6172%;" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"><strong title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">Description</span></strong></td><td class="highlight-grey" style="text-align: center;width: 5.7515%;" title="Background color : " data-highlight-colour="grey"><strong title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">Columns</span></strong></td><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" style="text-align: center;width: 4.54066%;" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"><strong title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">Availability</span></strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" style="width: 57.9042%;" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><p title=""><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Standard predefined tabular view definitions</span></strong></p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 8.99482%;"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Generic Table</span></p></td><td style="width: 38.6172%;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Standard predefined multi-purpose generic tabular view for element representation and modeling.</span></td><td><ul><li data-uuid="3a7ca169-8493-4be2-931b-a3ee9e8ca99c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Declared Name</span></li><li data-uuid="f28308b9-ea91-4a73-9c9a-cc76259ae575"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Declared Short Name</span></li><li data-uuid="b5ec87b6-6a43-41e5-b717-11d9828504be"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Documentation</span></li><li data-uuid="23651ec8-98a9-47ab-a62f-6c5045429f57"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Qualified Name</span></li><li data-uuid="3ac82e45-954d-4a30-8d68-b1453a81ab30"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Owner</span></li><li data-uuid="34fc12e5-c9f9-4211-aedf-194a771383b7"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Owned Element</span></li></ul></td><td style="width: 4.54066%;" rowspan="2"><ul style="text-align: left;"><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Via Textual Editor</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Via the Create View command</span></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 8.99482%;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Requirements Table</span></td><td style="width: 38.6172%;"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Standard predefined tabular view for requirements representation and modeling.</span></p><p>It subclassifies the <em>NonStandardLibraryElementFilter</em> view definition, which includes multiple filters that exclude various standard library elements that are unlikely to be relevant for display in views.</p></td><td><ul><li data-uuid="9c5e867b-ebca-4853-b342-48e4dcf17b1e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Declared Name</span></li><li data-uuid="84dc0f64-ced7-4dcb-9841-b7cecc5b358e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Req ID</span></li><li data-uuid="5f6fad88-c000-4c08-9bfe-23a55dda5ff2"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Documentation</span></li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Tabular view areas</h3><p>Views are displayed in the View Editor tab.</p><ul><li><strong><span class="confluence-link">Table toolbar </span></strong>allows you to quickly access the options for managing data in the table.<ul><li data-uuid="dad96f99-894c-4acf-a3ef-72531ae45a63">See the <ac:link ac:anchor="Tabular view toolbar buttons" /> page.</li></ul></li><li><strong>Grid</strong><span> </span>displays data according to the exposed elements and their filters.<ul><li data-uuid="659ad979-0436-4a00-b218-64ac45ea79e2">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in tabular views" /></ac:link> page.<br />The grid consists of:</li><li><strong>Rows </strong>that appear along the side of the table, and are labeled using numbers starting with 1. Each table<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">row represents an element</span><em>.<br /></em><ul><li data-uuid="601f75cd-7c95-4d14-a4cc-0e2ee8f5471b">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing tabular view rows" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></li><li><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="53608ec1-9cc2-4bc6-a081-14bc9fc7047b">Columns</ac:inline-comment-marker> </strong>that appear along the top of the table, and are <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c9f9ea1c-3b09-47fa-b5e3-18056e064aeb">labeled according to the specified metadata feature or named by hand.</ac:inline-comment-marker> Each table column<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">represents specific element data.</span><em><br /></em><ul><li data-uuid="1f28ea9b-b26b-4396-abd1-b01655db02d7">You can also modify tabular view columns.<ul><li data-uuid="b6083886-058e-4961-a364-a4112fda1d6c">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="aaed012b-b4c9-4c53-aeb0-92759f8fd2d3">Column cells can be <ac:link ac:anchor="Filtering tabular view column cells"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[sorted and filtered]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </li></ul></li><li><strong>Cells </strong>are the intersections of every row and column, which represent element data values.<ul><li data-uuid="4a38d167-08bd-494f-9842-bfedd5e8b0fc">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing tabular view cells" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></li></ul></li><li><strong>Table info area </strong>displays table information. </li></ul><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="table_areas.png" /></ac:image></strong></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Tabular view areas presented in a standard predefined tabular 'Generic Table' view.</h6><h4>Tabular view toolbar buttons</h4><p>The table below shows the buttons available in the tabular view toolbar.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Button</th><th scope="col">Name</th><th scope="col">Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="tabular_view_toolbar_refresh_button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Refresh</td><td>Click the button to update the displayed table content. </td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="tabular_view_toolbar_open_in_textual_editor_button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Open in Textual Editor</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click the button to open and highlight the table's textual notation in the Textual Editor. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f537dde-e663-4128-a49d-5eede52538c0"><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4a4cd048-db97-448c-a53e-cbab0b9abea0">In a</ac:inline-comment-marker> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Open in Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[temporary table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, the Textual Editor highlights all the content displayed in the table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="tabular_view_toolbar_add_new_button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Add New</td><td>Clicking the button displays a dropdown menu for selecting an element to be created in the model and displayed in the table. <ac:link ac:anchor="Adding elements to the table via the Add New command"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing tabular view rows" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="tabular_view_toolbar_delete_button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Delete</td><td>Clicking the button deletes the selected row elements from the table and the model. <ac:link ac:anchor="Deleting elements from the table via the Delete command"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing tabular view rows" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="tabular_view_toolbar_remove_from_table_button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Remove From Table</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Clicking the button removes the selected row elements from the table, but not from the model. This is achieved by filtering out the selected element from the table. <ac:link ac:anchor="Removing elements from the table via the Remove From Table command"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing tabular view rows" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5fbdd5c6-7515-47db-aed8-5fc8fc2d90a7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The button is unavailable in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Open in Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[temporary tables]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="tabular_view_toolbar_columns_button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Columns</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Clicking the button displays a dropdown menu for hiding/displaying table columns. It also contains a command for opening the <strong>Configure Table Columns</strong> dialog. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f7f68195-4888-4b00-9707-097a90aa10fb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The button is unavailable in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Open in Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[temporary tables]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="tabular_view_toolbar_options_button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Options</td><td>Clicking the button displays a dropdown menu for managing the table's display mode (e.g., Complete Tree, Compact Tree, List). <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006277 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00223: (2026x Refresh1) Terminate action

- page_id: `304006277`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006277/2026x+Refresh1+Terminate+action
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:19.255+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Behavior > (2026x Refresh1) Actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A terminate action usage is used to terminate the performance of some other action. The terminated action ends its performance by the completion of the terminate action usage. The terminated action may be given as an argument to the terminate action usage. If not given explicitly, by default, a terminate action usage terminates its own immediately containing action.

##### Specifying terminate actions

###### Specifying terminate actions via the Textual Editor

To specify terminate actions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the terminate action and do one of the following:
  1. To terminate the immediately containing action:
    1. For a named terminate action usage, declare the keyword action , specify the element name, and then the keyword terminate .
    2. For an unnamed terminate action usage, declare the keyword terminate .
  2. To terminate a non-immediately containing action:
    1. For a named terminate action usage, declare the keyword action , specify the element name, then the keyword terminate , followed by the name of the action you want to terminate.
    2. For an unnamed terminate action usage, declare the keyword terminate, followed by the name of the action you want to terminate.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

// ------ terminating the immediately containing action:

actionrun{ 
firststart; 
actionenable; 
thenactionproceed; 
thenterminate;// unnamed terminate action that terminates 'run' action 
} 
actionrunAction{ 
actionrun{ 
firststart; 
actionenable; 
thenactionproceed; 
thenactionterminateRunterminate;// named terminate action 'terminateRun' that terminates 'run' 
} 
}

// ------ terminating a non-immediately containing action:

actionrunAction{ 
actionrun{ 
firststart; 
actionenable; 
thenactionproceed; 
thenterminaterunAction;// unnamed terminate action that terminates 'runAction' 
} 
} 
actionrunAction{ 
actionrun{ 
firststart; 
actionenable; 
thenactionproceed; 
thenactionterminateRunActionterminaterunAction;// named terminate action 'terminateRunAction' that terminates 'runAction' 
} 
}

###### Specifying terminate actions via the view palette

To specify terminate actions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Actions group, click the **terminate** button to create a terminate action usage element.
2. Click on an action symbol in the view pane where you want to create the terminate symbol.

style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4 style="text-align: left;">Specifying terminate actions via the view palette</h4><p style="text-align: left;">To specify terminate actions via the view palette</p><hr style="text-align: left;" /><ol style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="20da99f3-d60e-46fe-96fc-5a70be0e7c3b">In the view palette, under the Actions group, click the<span> <strong>terminate </strong></span>button to create a terminate action usage element.</li><li data-uuid="a5d3c826-ca7c-43bd-980c-1e2a7afc8400">Click on an action symbol in the view pane where you want to create the terminate symbol.</li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004757 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00224: (2026x Refresh1) Textual Editor features

- page_id: `304004757`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304004757/2026x+Refresh1+Textual+Editor+features
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:17:44.368+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with Textual Editor > (2026x Refresh1) Managing Textual Editor
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the sections on this page for the Textual Editor features that can ease your workflow. See also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Command Palette' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Find dialog' space=''] pages.

##### Autocompletion

The Textual Editor provides an autocompletion function.

- Invoke the autocompletion dialog by pressing Ctrl+Space shortcut keys. Use the up and down keyboard buttons to navigate the suggestions and click the selection.

Use the **Preferred Element Name for Autocompletion**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Environment options' space=''] to specify which element name should be suggested in context-sensitive autocompletion.

Two types of autocompletion may be suggested in the autocompletion dialog:

- Snippets
  - Suggests keywords. Selecting the keyword creates the keyword with its notational structure.
  - Snippets are indicated with the square symbol. [ATTACHMENT filename='Autocompletion_1.png']
- Context-sensitive autocompletion
  - Suggests already-created elements' names and library packages.
  - Context-sensitive autocompletion is indicated with a box symbol. [ATTACHMENT filename='Autocompletion_2.png']

##### Folding the textual notation

Folding the element's body allows you to easily hide it in the textual notation if its visibility is not needed. It can be useful when the textual notation is extensive. The folding is only persistent while the Textual Editor is open; the element is unfolded upon the reopening of the Textual Editor.

To fold/unfold the textual notation

1. Locate the element whose body you want to fold.
2. Move the cursor to the left margin of the element keyword, next to the line number.
3. Do one of the following:
  1. Folding.
    1. Click the down-facing arrow to fold the body of an element. [ATTACHMENT filename='folding.png']
  2. Unfolding.
    1. Click the right-facing arrow to unfold it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
    2. Click the three dots next to the opening braces of the folded body. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can also hold Shift and click the arrow. If the selected element contains elements with bodies, the latter are folded upon the first click of the arrow (this applies only to the first level of elements with bodies). The second click folds the entire body of the selected element. Unfolding works the same way: the first click unfolds the selected element, and the second unfolds the contained elements with folded bodies.

##### Cross-navigation between transient and persistent models

If the Textual Editor is open while a model element is modified elsewhere in the model (e.g., its name is changed via the Containment tree), you can still target the element in the Textual Editor without synchronizing it. For example, using the Select in Containment Tree command from the Textual Editor will still select the correct element in the Containment tree, even if the Textual Editor does not display the updated name. However, to prevent potential model inconsistencies, concurrent editing is not recommended.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Textual Editor shortcut menu

Right-clicking anywhere in the Textual Editor pane opens the Textual Editor shortcut menu. The table below outlines the commands available in the shortcut menu.

| Command | Shortcut keys | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Go to Definition | Ctrl+F12 | Displays the definition of the selected keyword. |
| Go to Symbol... | Ctrl+Shift+O | Opens the Command Palette for element search by name. Type the element name you want to search for after the '@' symbol to navigate to the searched element in the textual notation. |
| Select in Containment Tree | Alt+B | Selects the element Containment tree. |
| Specification |  | Opens the Specification panel for the element. |
| Open View |  | Opens the view tab for the element. Available only for a view usage with a specified renderer. |
| Peek | Alt+F12 | Displays the Peek submenu:Peek Definition - displays the definition of the selected element. |
| Change All Occurrences | Ctrl+F2 | Selects all occurrences of the same selection. |
| Format Document | Shift+Alt+F | Formats the textual notation, such as alignment, spacing, etc. The command is disabled if the notation contains any errors. |
| Cut | Delete | Deletes the selection from the notation. |
| Copy | Ctrl+C | Copies the selection in the notation. |
| Paste | Ctrl+V | Pastes the copied selection in the notation. |
| Command Palette | F1 | Opens the Command Palette containing various Textual Editor navigation and editing commands. |

##### Textual Editor shortcut keys

The table below lists additional shortcut keys that can be used in the Textual Editor in addition to the shortcut keys indicated for the commands in the Textual Editor toolbar, the shortcut menu, and the Command Palette.

| Shortcut key | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Shift+Right | Selects characters one by one from the cursor to the right. |
| Ctrl+Shift+Right | Selects words one by one from the cursor to the right. |
| Ctrl+Shift+Left | Selects words one by one from the cursor to the left. |
| Alt+Up | Moves the line of text where the cursor is positioned up a line. |
| Alt+Down | Moves the line of text where the cursor is positioned down a line. |
| Shift+Alt+UpShift+Alt+Down | Copies the line of text where the cursor is positioned above or below the copied line. |
| Ctrl+Left | Jumps the cursor to the next word on the left. |
| Ctrl+Right | Jumps the cursor to the next word on the right. |
| Ctrl+F | Invokes the Find dialog. |
| Ctrl+Space | Invokes the autocompletion dialog. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006231 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00225: (2026x Refresh1) Textual notation import/export

- page_id: `304006231`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006231/2026x+Refresh1+Textual+notation+import+export
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:13.267+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with Textual Editor > (2026x Refresh1) Managing textual notation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can export SysML v2 project namespaces into *.sysml* textual notation files which you can later import into your projects.

The **Smart Generation Timeout** environment option allows you to specify the timeout duration for smart generation in seconds. During textual notation generation, the tool generates the shortest qualified names. If the timeout is specified, a simpler name is generated once the time limit is reached. Learn more on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Environment options' space=''] page.

##### Exporting textual notation

To export textual notation

1. Do one of the following:
  1. Export via File Menu:
    1. In the main menu, click File > Export To > SysML v2 Textual Notation . The Export SysML File window opens.
    2. In the Export SysML File window, in the All column, select the namespaces whose textual notation you want to export and click the Move [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
  2. Export via Containment tree:
    1. In the Containment tree, select the root namespace(s) whose textual notation you want to export.
    2. Right-click the selected namespace(s) and in the shortcut menu, click Tools > Export to SysML v2 Textual Notation . The Export SysML File window opens.
2. Review the selected namespace(s) in the Selected column.
3. In the Select folder section, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='select_folder_button.png'] button, and in the Select Folder window, select the location where the exported textual notation file will be saved. Click Select .
4. (Optional) Select the Delete existing .sysml files in the selected folder check box to delete all .sysml files in the output folder upon export.
5. Click Export . [ATTACHMENT filename='select_namespaces_window.png']

##### Importing textual notation

Import supports:

- Single file import
- Multiple file import at once
- Entire directory import

To import textual notation

1. In the main menu, click File > Import From > SysML v2 Textual Notation . The Select SysML v2 Files window opens.
2. In the Select SysML v2 Files window, select the .sysml file(s) you want to import and click Open . The selected file(s) are imported into separate root namespace(s).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can export SysML v2 project namespaces into <em>.sysml</em> textual notation files which you can later import into your projects.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="182bfe64-fdf3-42d3-bf47-1840ca677b0b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Smart Generation Timeout</strong> environment option allows you to <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">specify the timeout duration for smart generation in seconds. During textual notation generation, the tool generates the shortest qualified names. If the timeout is specified, a simpler name is generated once the time limit is reached. Learn more on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Environment options" /></ac:link> page.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 style="text-align: left;">Exporting textual notation</h3><p style="text-align: left;">To export textual notation</p><hr /><ol><li style="text-align: left;">Do one of the following:<ol><li>Export via File Menu:<ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Export To</strong> &gt; <strong>SysML v2 Textual Notation</strong>. The <strong>Export SysML File</strong> window opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Export SysML File</strong> window, in the <strong>All</strong> column, select the namespaces whose textual notation you want to export and click the <strong>Move <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Textual notation import/export" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong>button. </li></ol></li><li>Export via Containment tree:<ol><li style="text-align: left;">In the Containment tree, select the root namespace(s) whose textual notation you want to export.</li><li style="text-align: left;">Right-click the selected namespace(s) and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Tools </strong>&gt; <strong>Export to SysML v2 Textual Notation</strong>. The <strong>Export SysML File</strong> window opens.</li></ol></li></ol></li><li>Review the selected namespace(s) in the <strong>Selected</strong> column.</li><li>In the <strong>Select folder</strong> section, click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_folder_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Textual notation import/export" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><strong> </strong>button, and in the <strong>Select Folder</strong> window, select the location where the exported textual notation file will be saved. Click <strong>Select</strong>.</li><li>(Optional) Select the <strong>Delete existing .sysml files in the selected folder</strong> check box to delete all <em>.sysml</em> files in the output folder upon export.</li><li>Click <strong>Export</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_namespaces_window.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Textual notation import/export" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h3 style="text-align: left;">Importing textual notation</h3><p>Import supports:</p><ul><li data-uuid="eda8426c-b974-4194-9d0f-b257655aa98a">Single file import</li><li data-uuid="87a5e7b0-3da2-4839-8631-32b6f28ebc7f">Multiple file import at once</li><li data-uuid="31425d43-48d4-4bd8-a467-7cb47a550fb2">Entire directory import</li></ul><p style="text-align: left;">To import textual notation</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="2386fb53-2caa-448d-b40c-0fe43db2bcaf">In the main menu, click <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>SysML v2 Textual Notation</strong>. The <strong>Select SysML v2 Files </strong>window opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Select SysML v2 Files </strong>window, select the <em>.sysml</em> file(s) you want to import and click <strong>Open</strong>. The selected file(s) are imported into separate root namespace(s).</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314182507 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00226: (2026x Refresh1) Timeslice

- page_id: `314182507`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/314182507/2026x+Refresh1+Timeslice
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-07-01T14:53:35.364+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Common elements > (2026x Refresh1) Occurrences
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A timeslice is a type of suboccurrence representing a portion of the occurrence's lifetime.

##### Specifying or creating elements as timeslices

###### Specifying or creating elements as timeslices via the Textual Editor

To specify or create elements as timeslices via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, declare the *timeslice* keyword prior to the declaration of the element that you want to specify as a timeslice.
2. Click the **Synchronize**button. timeslicepartcarInMotion;

###### Specifying elements as timeslices via the shortcut menu

To specify elements as timeslices via the shortcut menu

- Right-click a usage element in the Containment tree or its symbol in a view and in its shortcut menu, in the Properties group, click **Portion Kind**, and in its submenu, click **timeslice** .

###### Creating elements as timeslices via the view palette

To create elements as timeslices via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, do one of the following:
  1. To create an occurrence usage timeslice or one that will correspond to the element kind for which the timeslice will be created:
    1. Click the**timeslice** button.
    2. Click in the view pane or the element symbol for which you want the timeslice to be created:
      1. Creating an element on the empty space of the view pane creates an occurrence usage element as a timeslice by default.
      2. Creating an element on another element symbol creates a timeslice for it:
        1. If the element symbol is a definition, the created timeslice is also a usage (e.g. part) that corresponds to the definition element for which the timeslice is created (e.g. part def).
        2. If the element is a usage, the created timeslice is also a usage of the same kind as the element for which the timeslice is created.
  2. To specify the timeslice's element kind:
    1. Click the down-facing arrow next to the timeslice button, and in its dropdown menu, click any timeslice... and select the specific element kind you need.
    2. Click in the view pane or the element symbol for which you want to create the timeslice.

If a timeslice element is created for an element specified as an individual, the timeslice is also created as an individual by default.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005694 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00227: (2026x Refresh1) Toolbars

- page_id: `304005694`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005694/2026x+Refresh1+Toolbars
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:43.976+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Project management > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling tool environment > (2026x Refresh1) User interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: Restoring default toolbars

INLINE

Toolbars help to speed up your work when performing commonly used tasks. Your modeling tool has two types of toolbars:

- Main toolbars - contain commands for basic project-related tasks and are located at the top of the modeling tool window, right under the main menu.
- View toolbars - contain commands for working with element symbols and are located at the top of the view pane.

To show or hide different toolbars

- In the main menu, click View , select either Main Toolbars , and in the displayed menu select/clear the check boxes of the toolbars that you want to show/hide.
- Right-click the desired toolbar area and in the shortcut menu select/clear the check boxes of the toolbars that you want to show/hide.

##### Customizing toolbars

You can customize existing toolbars by adding or removing buttons and also create new custom toolbars.

To add a new toolbar

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main menu, select View > Main Toolbars > Customize .
  - Right-click the toolbar area, and in the shortcut menu select Customize .
2. In the Customize Toolbars dialog, select the desired toolbar group and click the Add > New Toolbar .
3. Enter the name of the new toolbar and click OK .

After completing the above steps, a new toolbar is created in the selected toolbar group, and you can choose to show/hide it in the shortcut menu of the respective toolbar area. Note that the newly created toolbar does not have any buttons, so you need to add them after creating it.

To add a new button to a toolbar

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main menu, select View > Main Toolbars > Customize .
  - Right-click the toolbar area, and in the shortcut menu select Customize .
2. In the Customize Toolbars dialog, select the desired toolbar and click the Add > Button .
3. In the Add Button dialog, select the desired button or command and click OK .
4. Click OK one more time to close the Customize Toolbars dialog.

A new button is added to the selected toolbar. To add more than one button, repeat steps 2 and 3 as many times as you need.

To remove a toolbar or button

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main menu, select View > Main Toolbars > Customize .
  - Right-click the toolbar area, and in the shortcut menu select Customize .
2. In the Customize Toolbars dialog, select the toolbar or button you want to remove and click the Remove button.

After completing the above steps, the selected toolbar or button is removed from the respective toolbar area or toolbar. Toolbars that have been removed do not appear in the menu for selecting which toolbars you want to show.

#### NOTE: Restoring default toolbars

Restoring default toolbars

To undo all the commands for customizing toolbars, open the **Customize Toolbars** dialog, as described in step 1 above, and click the **Reset to Defaults** button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

To add more than one button, repeat steps 2 and 3 as many times as you need.</p><p><br /></p><p>To remove a toolbar or button</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the main menu, select <strong>View</strong> &gt; <strong>Main Toolbars</strong> &gt; <strong>Customize</strong>.</li><li>Right-click the toolbar area, and in the shortcut menu select <strong>Customize</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Customize Toolbars</strong> dialog, select the toolbar or button you want to remove and click the <strong>Remove</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />After completing the above steps, the selected toolbar or button is removed from the respective toolbar area or toolbar.
